findings.reachability: Admin-only: requires edit_post capability and nonce; data must first be saved via the metabox form
findings.justification: Saved outline data is written into an inline <script> block via json_encode() without JSON_HEX_TAG, JSON_HEX_APOS, or JSON_HEX_AMP flags; while upstream sanitize_text_field (strips tags) and esc_url_raw remove < > from stored values, making this not currently exploitable, the pattern is fragile and violates the defense-in-depth principle for JS context output.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: json_encode in script context without HEX flags
detection_pattern.description: Finds json_encode() output echoed directly into HTML/script contexts without JSON_HEX_TAG flag; without this flag, angle brackets are not Unicode-escaped, enabling </script> injection or HTML comment injection if upstream sanitization is ever relaxed or bypassed.

findings.reachability: Admin-only: requires edit_post capability and valid nonce on the dflip post edit screen
findings.justification: The else branch at line 751 executes `return ""` when an unexpected key (anything other than 'title' or 'dest') is encountered in a leaf node; this causes the entire function to return an empty string instead of continuing to sanitize remaining fields or simply skipping the unexpected key, meaning a specially-crafted array with an extra key causes the whole outline record to be discarded rather than sanitized — a sanitizer whose failure mode is silent data loss rather than clean rejection.

detection_pattern.section_name: Sanitizer: early return from sanitizer discards entire structure
detection_pattern.description: Finds sanitizer functions that return an empty string on unexpected keys instead of skipping/unsetting the key and continuing; this can cause the entire sanitized structure to silently collapse to empty on unexpected input, making sanitizer behavior unpredictable.

findings.reachability: Admin-only: exercised whenever a non-numeric outline destination is saved
findings.justification: sanitize_url() was deprecated in WordPress 6.1 in favor of sanitize_url() -> esc_url_raw(); while functionally equivalent today, use of deprecated functions creates maintenance risk and may behave differently under future WordPress versions.

detection_pattern.description: Detects use of the deprecated sanitize_url() function (deprecated WP 6.1+); plugins relying on it may break or behave unexpectedly under future WordPress versions.
